philosophy.title: A Co-pilot, not an Autopilot
philosophy.desc: Bayano is designed to assist you in writing your manuscript, not to do the work for you. The generated document is a base (a first draft) that requires your expertise, critical thinking, and revisions.
philosophy.warning: Warning: The generated content strictly requires your intervention.
philosophy.role1: Rigorous verification of facts, concepts, and generated sources.
philosophy.role2: Addition of your own critical thinking, analysis, and field data.
philosophy.role3: Adjustment of style, tone, and final formatting.

general.title: Academic Style
general.desc: Writing a thesis requires a formal, objective, and precise style. Avoid undefined jargon, colloquialisms, and the use of "I" (prefer the "we" of modesty or impersonal phrasing).
general.point1: Use technical vocabulary appropriate to your field.
general.point2: Write short and clear sentences (one idea per sentence).
general.point3: Ensure logical transitions between your paragraphs.

methodology.title: Developing an Academic Work
methodology.step1Desc: Choose a topic that you are passionate about but remains manageable. Delimit it precisely in time and space.
methodology.step2Desc: Use reliable sources: Google Scholar, Cairn, university libraries. Take systematic notes with their references.
methodology.step3Desc: Do not just describe. Ask a central question that your work will attempt to answer.
methodology.step4Desc: Do not wait until you have "finished everything" to write. Write as you go and proofread multiple times.

appUsage.title: How does Bayano work?
appUsage.intro: Discover the key steps to transform your idea into a structured manuscript. The application is interactive and allows you to stay in control at every step.
appUsage.step1Desc: Fill out the creation form accurately. The more detailed your instructions (context, problem statement, methodology), the more relevant and tailored the generated outline will be.
appUsage.step2Desc: The AI proposes an initial structure. You can drag and drop chapters, rename them, add new ones, or delete them. This outline is the backbone of your work.
appUsage.step3Desc: Generate content section by section. Use the AI Assistant (right panel) to rephrase paragraphs, elaborate on a specific point, or analyze an uploaded PDF document.
appUsage.step4Desc: Once the first draft is complete, export it to Word (.docx). This is where your real work as an author begins: correcting, enriching with your own data, and finalizing the layout.

citations.title: Citations & Footnotes
citations.desc1: Any idea borrowed from an author must be properly sourced to avoid plagiarism. The AI generates footnotes automatically, but it is your responsibility to verify their accuracy.
citations.desc2: Footnotes are also useful for defining a complex technical concept without cluttering the main text.

tables.title: Tables & Figures
tables.desc1: Tables help synthesize complex data (especially in empirical theses). They must always be commented on and introduced in the text.
tables.desc2: The AI will automatically generate this format. Upon Word or PDF export, these tags will be transformed into actual styled tables.

structure.title: Thesis Structure
structure.desc: An academic thesis follows a funnel structure: from general to specific.
structure.step1Desc: Context, problem statement, hypotheses, methodology, and outline announcement.
structure.step2Desc: Literature review, key concepts, and mobilized theories.
structure.step3Desc: Presentation of the field, analysis of results, and discussion.
structure.step4Desc: Summary, validation of hypotheses, limitations, and future directions.

wizard.title: New Manuscript
wizard.step1Desc: Let's start by defining the intellectual framework of your research.
wizard.step2Desc: Specify your methodological expectations and sources.
wizard.step3Desc: The AI will now structure your work.

placeholders.documentType: Ex: Guided tour report, Chemistry lab...
placeholders.title: Ex: The impact of artificial intelligence...
placeholders.subject: Ex: The impact of artificial intelligence on copyright in Europe...
placeholders.instructions: Ex: I want an empirical approach...
placeholders.instructionsCustom: Describe what you expect, the context, the key points to address...
placeholders.referenceText: Paste text excerpts, notes or a bibliography here...

wizard.step3Subtitle: Define the ambition and depth of your document.
wizard.adviceText: The AI will generate content section by section to ensure consistency.
wizard.adviceMemoire: A minimum of 60 pages is recommended for a Master's degree.
wizard.adviceArticle: A scientific article is generally between 10 and 20 pages.
wizard.adviceTp: A TP is generally between 5 and 15 pages depending on the subject.
wizard.adviceRapport: An internship report is generally between 30 and 50 pages.
wizard.modelDesc: Choose "Flash" for fast generation, or "Pro" for deeper but slower analysis.
wizard.structured: Structured (Generates a plan then writes by parts)
wizard.direct: Direct (Writes in one go without a plan)
wizard.tpModeDesc: The "Direct" mode is ideal for short or very specific documents.
wizard.editPlan: You can edit this plan before starting to write.

planEditor.title: Architecture of Knowledge
planEditor.subtitle: Refine the structure of your thought before starting the final writing.
planEditor.aiInstructions: Give instructions to the AI to reorganize or enrich your plan (e.g., "Add a chapter on economic impact", "Detail chapter 2 more", "Reorganize for a more sociological approach")
planEditor.addSectionIntro: Add a section to the Introduction

antiPlagiarism.title: Anti-Plagiarism & Paraphrasing
antiPlagiarism.subtitle: Detect AI-generated content and rephrase it to ensure the authenticity of your work.
antiPlagiarism.errorEmpty: Please enter text or upload a file.
antiPlagiarism.errorDocx: Error reading DOCX file. Make sure it is a valid Word file.
antiPlagiarism.errorFormat: Unsupported file format. Please use PDF or DOCX.
antiPlagiarism.aiDetectedDesc: This text shows strong characteristics of artificial intelligence generation.
antiPlagiarism.humanDetectedDesc: This text appears to have been written by a human.
antiPlagiarism.mixedDetectedDesc: This text contains a mix of human and AI-generated content.
antiPlagiarism.recommendationParaphrase: We recommend paraphrasing this text to reduce the likelihood of detection by anti-plagiarism tools.
antiPlagiarism.recommendationGood: This text is ready to be used as is.
antiPlagiarism.recommendationMixed: Consider rephrasing the parts that appear to be AI-generated.
antiPlagiarism.extractionNote: Note: Extraction focuses on raw text for analysis. Complex layout will not be displayed here.
antiPlagiarism.analyzingDesc: The AI is examining the linguistic structure, perplexity, and sentence variation of your document.
antiPlagiarism.paraphrasingDesc: Academic reformulation of the text to ensure its authenticity while retaining the original meaning.
antiPlagiarism.plagiarismRiskDesc: This text has a high probability of having been generated by an AI. We recommend using the paraphrasing tool to rephrase it and ensure the authenticity of your work.
antiPlagiarism.waitingAnalysisDesc: Paste your text or import a file, then click Analyze or Paraphrase to see the results here.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By accessing and using Bayano (the "Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use the Service.

description.title: 2. Description of Service
description.content: Bayano is an Artificial Intelligence-based writing assistant designed to help students and researchers structure, research, and write academic documents. The Service provides suggestions, outlines, and text drafts.

academicResponsibility.title: 3. Academic Responsibility and Anti-Plagiarism
academicResponsibility.content1: Bayano is an assistance tool (a "co-pilot") and does not replace the user's intellectual work in any way. The content generated by the AI must be considered as a draft or a working base.
academicResponsibility.content2: The user is solely and exclusively responsible for the final document submitted to their academic institution. By using Bayano, you acknowledge and agree that:
academicResponsibility.point1: Bayano declines all responsibility in the event of academic sanctions, manuscript rejection, loss of degree, or accusations of plagiarism related to the use of the Service.
academicResponsibility.point2: It is your responsibility to verify the accuracy of facts, citations, and bibliographic references generated by the AI (which may be subject to hallucinations).
academicResponsibility.point3: You must comply with your institution's internal regulations regarding the use of Artificial Intelligence.

acceptableUse.title: 4. Acceptable Use
acceptableUse.content: You agree to use the Service in a legal and ethical manner. It is strictly forbidden to:
acceptableUse.rule1: Use the Service to generate illegal, defamatory, hateful, or discriminatory content.
acceptableUse.rule2: Attempt to bypass technical limitations, hack, or disrupt the operation of the Service.
acceptableUse.rule3: Resell, sublicense, or commercially exploit the Service without explicit authorization.

limitation.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
limitation.content: To the fullest extent permitted by law, Bayano and its creators shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages resulting from the use or inability to use the Service, including but not limited to loss of data, loss of academic reputation, or service interruptions.
